hero_tagline: Supporting churches and organists across Surrey.
lede: Operating across much of Surrey, the SOA provides advice and support for organists — whatever their experience and level of expertise.

sections.title: Training new organists
sections.body: Our Organ Student Scheme is particularly suitable for pianists wishing to learn another instrument, and for those who have ‘reluctantly’ been asked to step in when no organist is available. The SOA provides an initial set of free-of-charge lessons, and a small grant towards music. For continuing students, we can suggest local teachers and provide further support through our education programme.

sections.title: Events — workshops and visits
sections.body: Our continuing education programme covers topics of interest to players of all levels in workshops directed by experienced musicians. Recent subjects include improvisation, hymn playing, practice techniques, and advice for new organists taking a first appointment. We also arrange visits for members to play organs in different locations, both within Surrey and further afield. See the full events page for the year ahead.

sections.title: Organ tuition at all levels
sections.body: Looking for regular lessons, or just some occasional help? We have a list of experienced tutors who will help with ABRSM grades, organ scholar applications, diploma exams, and basic hymn playing. Contact us for details.

sections.title: Looking for an organist?
sections.body: For temporary or permanent positions, we may be able to help. Our Find an organist page explains how to get in touch and what we can offer.

sections.title: Membership
sections.body: To join us and participate in our programme, please contact the Secretary (see below). Annual subscription is £13, payable on 1st October each year. New members joining after 1st April pay a half-rate of £6.50 for the remainder of that year. Student membership is £5 per year.

sections.title: Safeguarding
sections.body: The SOA has safeguarding procedures in place for occasions and events attended by young people or vulnerable adults. The policy is available on request from the Secretary.
